summary: A group of Dutch painters from Utrecht who traveled to Rome and adopted Caravaggio's chiaroscuro and tenebrism style in the early 17th century. Key members include Gerrit van Honthorst, Hendrik ter Brugghen, and Dirck van Baburen. Known for religious scenes with dramatic lighting and depictions of prostitutes and musicians.

works.name: General / Movement
works.description: The Utrecht Caravaggisti (also called the Utrecht School) were Dutch painters based in Utrecht who brought Caravaggio's style to the Netherlands. Key members: Gerrit van Honthorst (nicknamed 'Gherardo delle Notti' for his candlelit scenes), Hendrik ter Brugghen, and Dirck van Baburen. Named for the Dutch city of Utrecht and for the painter of The Calling of St. Matthew (Caravaggio). The movement also influenced later Dutch artists like Aelbert Cuyp's golden lighting in seascapes. Wikipedia

works.name: Gerrit van Honthorst
works.description: A leading Utrecht Caravaggist nicknamed 'Gherardo delle Notti' (Gerard of the Nights) for his use of small, visible light sources — notably candles — in his paintings. Key works include The Denial of St. Peter and Christ Before the High Priest. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Nicknamed 'Gherardo delle Notti' for his use of small visible light sources; The Denial of St. Peter and Christ Before the High Priest exemplify this technique
examples: The use of small visible light sources, as in The Denial of St. Peter and Christ Before the High Priest earned that artist the name 'Gherardo delle Notti'.

clues.clue: Painted a scene in which the view of a woman's candle is blocked by a guard's arm, while the woman holds the robe of the title man
examples: Another artist of this school painted a work in which the view of a woman's candle is blocked by a guard's arm, while the woman holds the robe of the title man.

works.name: Hendrik ter Brugghen
works.description: Utrecht Caravaggist known for paintings of musicians, including the Singing Lute Player and Flute Player. Also painted The Deposition, showing a pale hand pointing to a tool basket and a crown of thorns as three distraught women watch the burial; housed in Rome's San Pietro in Montorio. Wikipedia

works.name: Dirck van Baburen
works.description: Utrecht Caravaggist known for The Procuress, a depiction of prostitution. Wikipedia

comprehensive_summary:
The Utrecht Caravaggisti (also known as the Utrecht School) were Dutch painters from the city of Utrecht who traveled to Rome and absorbed the dramatic chiaroscuro lighting technique of Caravaggio, particularly his painting The Calling of St. Matthew. The movement's name combines the Dutch city of Utrecht with a reference to Caravaggio as its stylistic inspiration.

The three principal members are Gerrit van Honthorst, Hendrik ter Brugghen, and Dirck van Baburen. Van Honthorst earned the Italian nickname 'Gherardo delle Notti' (Gerard of the Nights) for his distinctive use of small, visible light sources such as candles. His key works include The Denial of St. Peter and Christ Before the High Priest, in which a woman's candle is obscured by a guard's arm as she holds the robe of the title figure. Ter Brugghen painted The Deposition (housed at San Pietro in Montorio in Rome), a scene where three distraught women witness the entombment as a pale hand points to a tool basket and crown of thorns in the lower corner; he also painted musician subjects such as the Singing Lute Player and Flute Player. Van Baburen contributed The Procuress to the school's notable corpus of paintings depicting prostitutes, another example of which is the Scene of Venal Love — a smiling, bare-chested woman grabbed by an old man.

The Utrecht School's influence extended to later Dutch artists: Aelbert Cuyp's golden lighting in seascape paintings such as The Maas at Dordrecht is attributed to the movement's influence.

cards.front: Movement: nicknamed 'Gherardo delle Notti' for use of small visible light sources; associated with The Denial of St. Peter and Christ Before the High Priest
Artist: painted a scene in which the view of a woman's candle is blocked by a guard's arm while she holds the robe of the title figure
Artist: painted The Deposition (San Pietro in Montorio, Rome), in which a pale hand points to a tool basket and crown of thorns as three distraught women watch the entombment
Artist: painted several musician subjects including the Singing Lute Player and Flute Player
Artist: painted The Procuress; member of a Dutch Baroque movement named for a city
Movement: noted for depictions of prostitutes including the Scene of Venal Love (smiling bare-chested woman grabbed by an old man) and The Procuress
Movement: influenced Aelbert Cuyp's golden lighting in seascape paintings like The Maas at Dordrecht
